decontamination_notes: Real bug fix from npm/node-semver PR #874, merged 2026-06-09, after model training cutoffs. When expanding an x-range, node-semver accepted a fixed (non-wildcard) segment following an x/* wildcard segment (e.g. 1.x.5 or x.1), silently coercing it into a garbage range instead of rejecting it. The fix (classes/range.js) adds an invalidXRangeOrder(M, m, p) predicate and, in replaceXRange, returns the comparator unexpanded when a wildcard is followed by a fixed segment, so validRange returns null. Repo sliced at the PR base commit (6b77aa84); the whole node-semver library is included so require('./index') resolves. Pure JavaScript, zero external dependencies; hidden tests run under Node's built-in node:test (no image needed). ISC LICENSE preserved. Coverage floor: 3 fail_to_pass tests (fixed patch after x minor, fixed minor after x major, fixed patch after x major+minor), each producing a non-null garbage range at base and null after the fix, plus a pass_to_pass regression guard over well-formed trailing-x ranges that expand identically at base and after the fix.

fail_to_pass.name: fixed_patch_after_x_minor
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb xrange fixed patch after x minor$' vb_xrange_order.test.js

fail_to_pass.name: fixed_minor_after_x_major
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb xrange fixed minor after x major$' vb_xrange_order.test.js

fail_to_pass.name: fixed_patch_after_x_major_minor
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb xrange fixed patch after x major minor$' vb_xrange_order.test.js
